user_query:
I’m preparing two situation-specific de-escalation playbooks for my team and family. Please read the scenarios in input/scenarios.json and produce a single structured plan at output/plan.json that meets all of the following requirements.

General output file:
- Write exactly one JSON file to output/plan.json
- Top-level keys and types must be:
  {
    "plan_version": "1.0",
    "scenarios": {
      "angry_retail_customer": { /* object per schema below */ },
      "family_escalation": { /* object per schema below */ }
    },
    "global_safety_rules": [ /* array of strings */ ]
  }

Per-scenario schema and constraints (apply to both scenarios):
- threat_assessment: object with keys and non-empty strings:
  - hands
  - stance
  - face
  - voice
  - context
  - threat_level: one of "LOW", "MEDIUM", "HIGH"
- body_positioning: object with:
  - angle: must explicitly include "45" (e.g., "45-degree angle")
  - reactionary_gap_ft: number >= 6
  - hands: must include the words "open" and "palms"
  - feet: must include the word "balls" (as in weight on the balls of your feet)
  - exit_strategy: non-empty string that preserves your path to an exit
- voice_and_tone: object with all of the following exact intent values (the string can include more detail but must contain these cues):
  - volume_start: must mention matching at ~70% (e.g., "~70% then taper")
  - pace: must mention "half" their speed/pace
  - pitch: must include "low"
  - tone: must include "respectful" or "non-confrontational"
- verbal_techniques: object with:
  - acknowledgment: a validating statement tailored to the scenario
  - open_questions: array with at least 2 scenario-relevant questions
  - paraphrase: a paraphrase reflecting the person’s core concern
  - limited_choices: offer exactly 2 concrete options (e.g., "I can do X or Y for you. Which works better?")
  - boundary_statement: a calm boundary framed as collaboration (not a threat)
- prohibited_phrases_used: boolean (must be false). Also, do not include any of these prohibited phrases anywhere in the plan: "Calm down", "You need to relax", "That's not a big deal", "There's nothing I can do", "You're being unreasonable", "It's policy".
- pre_attack_indicators_to_watch: array with at least 3 items chosen from this set: ["target glance", "thousand-yard stare", "blading", "sudden silence", "fists clenching", "shoulders rise", "removing glasses/hat/jacket", "closing distance despite step-backs"].
- scenario_protocol: object with:
  - name: a short label for the scenario type
  - steps: array of at least 3 short, concrete actions appropriate to the scenario
- disengage_criteria: array with at least 2 clear, observable conditions under which to stop engaging and leave (e.g., repeated closing distance after two step-backs, thousand-yard stare, etc.)

Scenario-specific requirements:
- angry_retail_customer:
  - scenario_protocol.name must include "retail" or "food service"
  - At least one step must reference using the counter as a barrier (e.g., "stay behind the counter")
  - At least one step must mention involving/"get a manager" if the customer won’t de-escalate after attempts
- family_escalation:
  - scenario_protocol.name must include "family"
  - Include the exact phrase "You might be right" somewhere within verbal_techniques (acknowledgment or paraphrase is fine)
  - At least one step must change the environment (e.g., step outside or get fresh air on the balcony)

Global rules:
- global_safety_rules must include all of the following strings (you can include others as well):
  - "Your personal safety is the top priority"
  - "Never block someone's exit path"
  - "Keep hands visible; do not touch an agitated person unless they are in immediate danger"
  - "Do not attempt to physically restrain someone unless trained"

Use the concrete observations and context in input/scenarios.json to tailor each section. Do not include any prohibited phrases. Ensure output/plan.json is valid JSON and adheres exactly to the key names and structural requirements above.
